title: Find Next Smaller value in Linked List

content.problemStatement:
Chef gave you the headheadhead of a linked list and challenged you to find the next smaller value for every node in the linked list. Can you solve the challenge?

**Note:** If for some value, there is no next smaller value then assume the next smaller value for this number to be −1-1−1.


Input Format

First-line will contain TTT, the number of test cases. Then the test cases follow.
Each test case contains two lines of input.
The first line of every test case contains an integer NNN - the length of array.
The second line of every test case contains NNN integers - A1,A2,..,AN A_1,A_2,..,A_NA1​,A2​,..,AN​ denoting the integers in the array.
You don't need to read or print anything. Just complete the function nextSmallerValue() which takes the head of the linked list as input.


Output Format
Return the head of the linked list which contains next smaller value for every node.
constraints: Sum of N over all test cases does not exceed 5*10^5
content.inputFormat: The first line contains an integer T, the number of test cases. For each test case, the first line contains an integer N, the length of the linked list. The second line contains N space‑separated integers representing the node values from head to tail.
content.outputFormat: For each test case output a single line containing N space‑separated integers. The i‑th integer is the value of the next node to the right that is strictly smaller than the i‑th node; if no such node exists output -1.

sampleTestCases.explanation: Test case 1: No element to the right is smaller than 9 or 19, so both are -1.\nTest case 2: Each element's next smaller value is the first strictly smaller element to its right; the last two 8's have none, so -1.\nTest case 3: 14 → 9, 9 → 5, second 14 → 5, 5 → 2, 2 → 1, 1 → -1.

solution.approach: The problem is equivalent to finding the Next Smaller Element to the right for each array element. Scan the array from right to left while maintaining a monotonic increasing stack (top holds the smallest candidate). For each element a[i]: pop stack elements that are >= a[i]; if the stack becomes empty, there is no smaller element to the right, so answer[i] = -1; otherwise answer[i] = stack.top(). Finally push a[i] onto the stack. This runs in O(N) per test case because each element is pushed and popped at most once. The linked list is only a conceptual wrapper; we work directly on the array of values.
solution.timeComplexity: O(N) per test case, where N is the length of the linked list. Overall O(∑N) ≤ 5·10^5.
solution.spaceComplexity: O(N) for the answer array and the stack.

code.Python3:
import sys

def solve():
    data = sys.stdin.read().strip().split()
    if not data:
        return
    it = iter(data)
    T = int(next(it))
    out_lines = []
    for _ in range(T):
        N = int(next(it))
        a = [int(next(it)) for _ in range(N)]
        ans = [-1] * N
        stack = []  # will store values
        for i in range(N-1, -1, -1):
            while stack and stack[-1] >= a[i]:
                stack.pop()
            ans[i] = -1 if not stack else stack[-1]
            stack.append(a[i])
        out_lines.append(' '.join(map(str, ans)))
    sys.stdout.write('\n'.join(out_lines))

if __name__ == "__main__":
    solve()
